parent.label: Wikipedia
parent.description: The encyclopedia that trained almost every language model that exists. Wikipedia is written in a distinctive neutral, encyclopedic register — 'X was born in Y and is known for Z' — that has influenced the default voice of AI more than any other single source. It is also available in 300+ languages.
bullets: English Wikipedia: 6.7M articles, 4.4B words — heavily upweighted in most training runs
Available in 300+ languages, making it the primary signal for multilingual models
The 'Wikipedia voice' — neutral, definitional, present-tense — is now the default AI register

cells.label: English Wikipedia
cells.description: 6.7 million articles covering everything from subatomic particles to obscure 19th-century politicians to every episode of The Simpsons. Heavily upweighted in most training runs because of its density, consistency, and citation discipline.
bullets: The Pile allocates ~17% to Wikipedia — dramatically more than its internet share
Infoboxes, citation templates, and category trees provide structured metadata
Talk pages and edit histories reveal the social construction of 'neutral' knowledge
cells.screenshotCaption: English Wikipedia — the single most upweighted source in most training pipelines

cells.label: Other Languages
cells.description: Wikipedia in 300+ other languages — from Mandarin (1.4M articles) to Tamil (160,000+ articles) to Scots (60,000). These provide the multilingual foundation that allows models to operate in non-English contexts, though data quality and volume vary enormously by language.
bullets: Tamil Wikipedia: 160,000+ articles; Tamil has 80M+ speakers but far less web presence than its speaker count suggests
Low-resource languages are systematically underrepresented, even in Wikipedia
Multilingual Wikipedia teaches cross-lingual alignment — the same fact in different languages
cells.screenshotCaption: Tamil Wikipedia — representing one of 300+ language editions included in training
